profile.name: Manikyam Golla
profile.role: Looking for an Internship or Entry-Level Role — Available Now
profile.tagline: From ideas to interactive web experiences.
bio: I'm a final-year B.Tech student in Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning at Vijaya Institute of Technology for Women, graduating in 2027. I care about building things that actually work end-to-end — from an idea to a real interface someone can use.

My work spans the fundamentals of supervised and unsupervised learning and basic NLP, plus web development with Python, HTML, and CSS. I've backed that up with certifications from Infosys, Intel, Google, and IBM.

I learn fastest by shipping. Several projects in, including an IoT smart-irrigation system that won a Project Expo and two hackathon-winning web apps, I'm looking for an internship or entry-level role where I can keep building.

certifications.name: The Joy of Computing using Python

projects.name: Fake News Detection Using Machine Learning
projects.tagline: An NLP classifier that flags news articles as authentic or fabricated, deployed with an interactive UI.
overview: Misinformation spreads faster than most people can fact-check it. I wanted hands-on experience with a full NLP classification pipeline, from raw text to a usable interface.

The pipeline cleans and stems article text, extracts TF-IDF features, and feeds them into a trained classifier. I deployed it behind a Streamlit UI so anyone can paste in an article and get an instant read.
challenges: Noisy, inconsistent article text across sources
Balancing precision vs. recall so the classifier isn't overconfident on ambiguous articles
learnings: How much preprocessing quality affects a classical NLP pipeline's ceiling
TF-IDF plus a well-tuned classifier is a strong, lightweight baseline before reaching for deep learning
projects.results: Classifier reliably distinguishes clear-cut fake vs. real articles in testing, deployed as an interactive demo.

projects.name: Gradify — GPA & CGPA Calculator
projects.tagline: A precision-driven GPA/CGPA calculator that streamlines academic performance tracking for students.
overview: Most students track GPA in scattered spreadsheets. Gradify was built at a hackathon to give students one clean, fast tool for both semester and cumulative GPA tracking.
It won the hackathon on the strength of its usability — simple inputs, instant accurate output, and an interface that doesn't get in the way.
challenges: Handling varied university grading scales in one flexible calculation model
Building a polished UI under hackathon time pressure
learnings: How to scope a small tool tightly enough to finish and polish it in a time-boxed event
The outsized value of a clean UI when judges are comparing many similar tools
projects.results: Won its category at the hackathon; used by classmates for real semester tracking.

projects.name: Give Hope — Donation Platform
projects.tagline: A web platform connecting donors with verified charitable causes for transparent, secure giving.
overview: Trust is the biggest barrier to online giving. Give Hope was built to connect donors directly with verified charities, with transparency built into every listing.
The stack is a standard MERN setup — React frontend, Express/Node backend, MongoDB for storage — chosen to move fast for the hackathon while staying production-realistic.
challenges: Modeling NGO verification in a way that's trustworthy without a real-world verification pipeline behind it yet
Building a secure-feeling checkout flow within a hackathon timeframe
learnings: End-to-end MERN development under real time pressure
How much UI trust signals (verification badges, transparency) matter for donation products specifically
projects.results: Won its hackathon category for addressing a real trust gap in online donation platforms.

projects.name: Medi Slot — Healthcare Appointment Booking
projects.tagline: A booking platform that connects patients with providers and keeps scheduling friction-free.
overview: Booking a doctor's appointment still means phone calls and guesswork for a lot of patients. Medi Slot gives patients a direct, searchable way to find a provider and book a slot without the back-and-forth.
Built on a MERN stack, it pairs a patient-facing booking flow with a scheduling model on the backend so provider availability stays accurate as bookings come in.
challenges: Keeping doctor availability accurate in real time as multiple patients book concurrently
Designing a booking flow simple enough for non-technical patients
learnings: How to model time-slot availability so double-bookings can't happen
Prioritizing a low-friction UI for a genuinely mixed-skill user base
projects.results: Working end-to-end booking flow from doctor search through confirmed appointment.

projects.name: Hydro Hive — Smart Irrigation System
projects.tagline: An IoT system that monitors soil moisture and automates watering to conserve water in real time.
overview: Over-watering and under-watering both waste resources and hurt crop yield. Hydro Hive automates that decision using live soil-moisture data instead of a fixed schedule.

Built as a team project with embedded sensors feeding real-time moisture readings into logic that triggers irrigation only when needed — it won Project Expo for its practical, measurable water savings.
challenges: Getting consistent, noise-free readings from soil-moisture sensors
Tuning thresholds so irrigation triggers matched real crop needs
learnings: Hands-on embedded systems work: sensor calibration, firmware logic, hardware-software integration
How to present a hardware project's impact in terms judges (and non-engineers) immediately understand
projects.results: Won the Project Expo; demonstrated clear reduction in unnecessary watering during live testing.
